_meta.description: Core ElevarusOS tables (not integration-owned). Integration tables live in each src/integrations/<name>/manifest.ts and are merged at boot by src/core/integration-registry.ts. Consumed by data-tools.ts describe_schema and the supabase_query whitelist.

jobs.description: One row per orchestrated workflow job (blog, report, etc.). Stage history, status, and approval state all live here.

columns.status: queued | running | awaiting_approval | approved | failed | completed.

request.description: Full job request JSONB (title, brief, audience, keyword, cta, etc.).

stages.description: Array of StageRecord objects — ordered by execution. Heavy.

approval.description: ApprovalState JSONB { required, approved, approvedBy, approvedAt, notes }.

instances.description: Snapshot of every bot instance config, synced from src/instances/<id>/instance.md at ElevarusOS startup.

brand.description: InstanceBrand JSONB { voice, audience, tone, industry }.

synced_at.description: When the instance config was last synced from disk.

job_stages_view.description: Denormalized view of the jobs.stages JSONB array — one row per stage run. Use this for stage-level analytics ('which stage fails most?') without loading full job objects. Stage outputs are NOT included — join to jobs.stages for outputs.

job_created_at.description: When the parent job was created.

columns.stage_index: Ordinal position of this stage within the job (1-based).
columns.error: Stage-level error message (if any). Internal use only.
columns.has_output: True if the stage produced output (fetch via jobs.stages for the body).

instance_job_summary.description: Rollup view — per-instance job counts (total, completed, failed, active) + last run timestamps. Good for dashboard cards and quick health checks.

ask_elevarus_queries.description: Audit log for every Slack Q&A tool invocation. Useful for debugging tool selection, latency, error patterns, and per-user query volume.

params.description: Input params passed to the tool. Heavy.

columns.total_available: Rows the underlying query would have returned (for capped queries).
columns.slack_user_id: Slack user who triggered the tool.
columns.slack_channel_id: Slack channel the event came from.
columns.trace_id: Correlation id (matches the Slack event_id when available).
